key_insight: The paper extends Perron--Frobenius theory and eigenvector centrality from real nonnegative networks to complex-weighted networks, enabling node importance measures when edge weights have phase as well as magnitude.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers and scientists a principled way to rank nodes in systems with complex interactions, including quantum, circuit, chemistry, and communication models.
method: Generalize PF-style conditions and compare related complex-matrix formulations.
Define eigenvector-based centralities for complex-weighted networks.
Test the theory on example networks from transport, circuits, chemistry, and communications.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical existence results plus computed centrality values on several illustrative example networks, rather than a large empirical benchmark.
watch_item: Complex eigenvectors may be harder to interpret than standard centralities, so implementation must handle phase ambiguity and normalization carefully.
paper_title: Generalizing Perron--Frobenius theory and eigenvector-based centralities to networks with complex edge weights
